tenors.narrative: Markets show minimal conviction on near-term direction with a median of $67,700, essentially flat to spot across 36 markets. The interquartile range ($66,700–$68,917) reflects modest volatility expectations, with a 10th percentile at $66,100 and 90th percentile at $69,704.

A notable downside outlier emerges with median at $64,600 (-4.6%), suggesting concentrated bearish positioning in this single-day bucket across 36 markets. The wide 90th percentile of $70,873 indicates significant disagreement, though the 25th percentile clusters near $64,267.

Markets revert to near-spot expectations with a median of $67,821 (+0.1%) across 38 markets and double the volume at $8.7M, signaling renewed equilibrium. The wider distribution (10th=$64,188, 90th=$72,286) reflects elevated uncertainty around this April tenor.

Prediction markets show modest upside bias at $67,846 (+0.2% median) with tight upper-tail convergence at $68,000 (both 75th and 90th percentiles), suggesting consensus on a capped upside scenario across 38 markets.

Markets remain anchored near spot at $67,737 (-0.0% median) with a notably wide 90th percentile of $73,289, indicating tail-risk positioning for potential rallies despite the interquartile concentration around $65,105–$70,622.

Similar positioning to prior dates with median at $67,886 (+0.2%), but notable clustering at $70,000 ceiling for both 75th and 90th percentiles, suggesting structured resistance expectations in the $70K region.

Severe downside pricing dominates long-dated contracts with a -32.1% median at $46,000, reflecting elevated tail-risk hedging or bearish structural views. The compressed interquartile range ($40,000–$55,000) and zero active markets indicate this bucket lacks price discovery and may reflect stale or sparse positioning.

tenors.narrative: With 29 markets and substantial $11.7M volume, prediction markets show modest upside consensus for March 2026, with a median of $103 (+2.4%) and tight interquartile range ($101-$107), suggesting relatively stable prices near current levels with limited conviction for directional moves.

April 2026 predictions show dramatic bullish expectations with a median of $125 (+24.4%), though this forecast carries lower credibility given only 2 markets and minimal $90k volume, with very wide price dispersion ($67-$150) indicating substantial uncertainty.

June 2026 exhibits elevated uncertainty with 13 markets predicting a median decline to $84 (-16.8%), but the extremely wide distribution ($54-$200) and inverted quartiles suggest fragmented market views, with thin $788k volume limiting confidence in any directional bias.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal upside expectation for Q1 2026, with a median of $4,555 (+0.5%) across 22 markets. The 80% confidence interval spans $4,340-$4,770, suggesting relatively tight consolidation near current levels over the next 15 months.

Market participants are materially divided on H1 2026 gold prices, with a median forecast of $4,034 (-11.0%) but an exceptionally wide 80% confidence interval of $3,800-$6,333 across 13 markets. This bimodal distribution reflects significant uncertainty around macro drivers, with tail risks extending to both sub-$3,800 and above-$6,300 outcomes.
